essence: Extended dogfooding review of Captain's Log flags read-only markdown (needs a show button), dark-mode-only theme, transcript vocabulary miscorrections (Carbonetics vs Kybernesis), ungoverned/missing tags needing a centralized future-proof tag store, empty noise captures showing by default, and the daily manual Plaud token repair as the priority fixes.

actions.text: Add a show-markdown/show-transcript button and a light theme to the capture view
Route all transcripts and synopses through a vocabulary correction system keyed to David's real product names
Automate the Plaud refresh-token refetch (currently a manual browser-console paste), likely via Playwright
Analyze the kybernesis.ai design system and produce a DESIGN.md under brand-dave
Centralize tag storage/governance so missing tags (captains-log, kybernesis, lars) are fixed future-proof, possibly a dedicated tag-management app
Hide empty/noise captures by default behind a toggle and add semantic source coloring (OMI green, Plaud blue)

essence: Captures in Captain's Log need attachable actions, notes, and done-marking — possibly via a multi-conversational observation audit log collected while using the app and consolidated in one pass.

Ingestion items have no connective tissue — no linear temporal chain linking the many conversations on one topic, and no quest/side-quest thematic threads (Dark Factory vocabulary) for when one conversation forks into several idea chains.

loop.label: temporal and thematic linking of captures

essence: Two-agent feedback loop for the Kyber Extension SDK: an extension-builder agent (fed by the KyberAgent brain agent) develops extensions through the standard SDLC, and when it hits missing host/harness/daemon capabilities it prioritizes and files roadmap tickets for a second agent that extends the Kybernesis platform itself.

Stand up a dedicated KyberAgent brain agent (covering the extension SDK, daemon, and harness) on Roamy using its $100 credits — it should read all KDD docs and commits and write David a recurring report on what Ian and Martin are adding across the whole Kybernesis system, mirroring Ian's multi-agent research→design→build→deploy workflow.

actions.text: Build the first agent — a KyberAgent-knowledge brain agent — on Roamy using the $100 credits
Have that agent read KDD docs and commits and write a recurring report on new KyberAgent/Kybernesis changes by Ian and Martin

loop.label: learn KyberAgent via dedicated brain agent

essence: Reset Dark Factory planning: replace the wall-of-text roadmap with a conversational one-question-at-a-time Q&A walkthrough, and elevate the complex constellation applications onto the roadmap as sequenced one-at-a-time builds instead of piecemeal tasks.

essence: Captain's Log ingestion must be durable beyond the web server's lifetime — launchd-style cron every 30 minutes from 07:00 to 22:00 plus an in-app manual trigger — with slice-and-dice filter toggles (tags, timeframe, keyword search, noise hidden by default) and the build verified against the soon-publishable Kyber Extension SDK.

actions.text: Make OMI/Claude transcript ingestion a durable launchd-style job (30-min cadence, 07:00-22:00) with a manual trigger in the app
Add filter toggles for tags, timeframe, keyword search, and noise (hidden by default)
Verify the tool's architecture fits the Kyber Extension SDK and import it once publishable

essence: Batch-triage the weeks-long backlog of affiliate reach-outs (mostly AI-TLDR) in the david@ideasmen inbox — classify scammy vs real, check whether David had initiated contact and forgotten, and pre-draft responses; per a later note, to be done together with Jan and Mary.

essence: Plaud auth is brittle — using Plaud from another client (Android/web) rotates the refresh token — so Captain's Log needs an easy re-fetch flow that stores the renewed token against the Plaud ingestion provider.

actions.text: Build an easy refresh-token re-fetch plus storage against the Plaud ingestion provider

essence: Open question: where should raw Plaud and OMI transcripts land (current folder vs the second brain vs a TIL-style process, which exists for OMI but maybe not Plaud) — and the Captain's Log app needs its own ingestion-destination configuration.

actions.text: Add ingestion-destination configuration for Plaud/OMI raw transcripts

loop.label: ingestion data destinations and app config

essence: Architecture pass for the Captain's Log Kybernesis extension (Plaud+OMI): decide the server foundation (AppyStack vs what Kybernesis already provides), normalize David's vernacular in transcripts without changing context (AppyDave vs Happy Dave), list-view UX with hover metadata/markdown/clipboard/search, per-provider cron configured in the extension but owned by the host daemon, and spin tickets for missing SDK/host capabilities so extension-built functionality is offloaded to the platform later.

actions.text: Mine prior Claude conversations on captains-log/OMI/Plaud into a detailed requirements doc and spec, then build the extension
Raise Kybernesis tickets for missing SDK/host capabilities (e.g. daemon-managed cron invoking extension hooks) and refactor the extension to offload them later
